seoTitle: How to Add Video to Brevo Email Campaigns
metaDescription: Boost click-through rates by embedding video in Brevo emails using the native video content block, thumbnails, and play button customization.
intro: Bulky video attachments can trigger spam filters, and writing custom HTML embed code is more effort than most campaigns need. Brevo's drag-and-drop editor includes a dedicated video content block that automatically generates a clickable thumbnail, giving you a rich multimedia experience without the technical overhead.

steps.heading: Open the campaign designer
steps.body: Go into your email campaign's drag-and-drop editor where you'll be building out the layout.

steps.heading: Drag in the video content block
steps.body: Locate the specialized video block in the elements panel and drop it into your email body where you want the video to appear.

steps.heading: Add your video link
steps.body: Paste the link to your video so Brevo can automatically fetch a high-quality preview thumbnail.

steps.heading: Customize the thumbnail and play button
steps.body: Swap the automatic placeholder thumbnail for a custom-branded static graphic or animated GIF, and adjust the play button overlay if needed.

steps.heading: Set alt text and test responsiveness
steps.body: Add alternative text for accessibility, adjust padding alignment, and test how the video block displays across mobile layouts before sending.

faq.question: Does the video actually play inside the email?
faq.answer: No, clicking the thumbnail typically redirects recipients to view the video on an external page rather than playing it natively inside most email clients.

faq.question: Can I use a custom thumbnail instead of the automatic one?
faq.answer: Yes, you can swap the auto-generated thumbnail for your own branded static image or animated GIF.

faq.question: Will adding video hurt my email deliverability?
faq.answer: Using a linked thumbnail rather than a bulky attached video file avoids the deliverability issues large attachments can trigger.

faq.question: Does the video block work on mobile email clients?
faq.answer: Yes, but it's worth testing your specific layout on mobile since responsive behavior can vary by device.
